# enable-cli-models
Description: Enable specific models for use through a CLI or hardcoded list by updating the system's model catalog.
Source: project
1. Identify the models that need to be made available via a command-line interface (CLI) or a predefined internal list.
2. Locate the system file responsible for managing the model catalog. This file typically contains functions or data structures that define available models and their properties.
3. Find the specific function or section within this file that handles the refresh or update process for models accessed through a CLI or hardcoded list.
4. Modify this function or data structure to include or explicitly enable the desired models. Ensure their availability status is set correctly (e.g., `is_available: 1`).
5. Rebuild the application or system to incorporate the changes made to the source file.
6. Trigger a refresh of the model catalog within the system. This might be an explicit command or an automatic process upon restart.
7. Verify that the newly enabled models are listed as available in the system's model selection interface or database.
